title: Fixing Broken Nested HTML Structure with Invalid Headings

statement.markdown: Given a string representation of an HTML document, validate that all headings (h1-h6) are properly nested and have valid tag names. A valid heading has a corresponding closing tag and does not contain any other HTML elements or attributes. The function should return true if the HTML structure is valid, false otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing an HTML document
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the HTML structure is valid
statement.notes: Pay attention to the nesting of headings and the validity of tag names.

constraints.description: The number of HTML elements in the document

examples.explanation: Invalid nesting: a paragraph is present between headings

hints.text: Start by parsing the HTML string and keeping track of the current heading level

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing Broken Nested HTML Structure
editorial.intuition: We need to keep track of the current heading level and validate that it matches the expected level based on the tag name.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: manually parse the HTML string and check for valid nesting and tag names.
editorial.betterSolution: Use a stack to keep track of the current heading level and validate that it matches the expected level.
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterate over the HTML string and use a state machine to track the current heading level and validate that it matches the expected level.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution correctly validates the HTML structure and handles cases with invalid nesting and tag names.
editorial.dryRun:
Example run: given the input '

Heading 1

Heading 2

Heading 1

', the optimal solution returns True.
commonMistakes: Not handling cases with invalid nesting and tag names
Not using a state machine to track the current heading level
